body_md:
**Pre-Market Edition · Monday Open**

> **DATA TRANSPARENCY:** Price levels sourced from live searches (Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, StocksToTrade, Investing.com). Live options-chain data (exact bid/ask, live greeks) is **unavailable** — premium ranges and IV are estimated from historical ranges and pre-earnings context. Technical indicators (VWAP, exact MAs) are projected from available price action. All recommendations are based on confirmed price data only.

## Section 1 — Macro Market Context

| Indicator | Reading | Signal |
|---|---|---|
| **SPY** | $715.17 (+0.17%) | Flat / Slight Bid |
| **QQQ** | $664.23 (−0.01%) | Flat / Neutral |
| **VIX** | 18.02 | Moderate — Elevated Pre-Event |
| **S&P 500** | ~7,150 | Above 7,000 — Structurally Bullish |
| **Market Breadth** | Positive A/D Line | Broadly Constructive |
| **Nasdaq Futures** | Flat/Slightly Bid | No Strong Directional Bias |
| **Market Posture** | Pre-Earnings Superweek + Pre-FOMC | **ELEVATED CAUTION** |

### Critical Macro Calendar This Week

| Day | Event |
|---|---|
| **Monday Apr 28 (TODAY)** | Dallas Fed Manufacturing 10:30 AM — Pre-earnings drift |
| **Tuesday Apr 29 (TOMORROW)** | GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META earnings after close |
| **Wednesday Apr 30** | **FOMC Rate Decision** + AAPL, SNDK earnings after close |

**Macro conclusion:** Market is flat-to-slightly-bid with VIX at 18. The backdrop is constructive BUT loaded: the biggest earnings week of 2026 (Mag 7 + SNDK) intersects with a Fed decision. IV is inflated across all pre-earnings names. This limits clean 0DTE setups. **Only trade relative-strength outliers with high-conviction intraday setups. Reduce position size 30–40% from normal.** Broad-index calls (SPY/QQQ) require a clear breakout above resistance — do not chase flat opens.

## Section 2 — Individual Ticker Analysis

### AAPL — Apple Inc.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Premarket Price | **$273.33** |
| Prev Close | ~$266.14 |
| Premarket Gap | **+$7.19 / +2.69%** |
| PM High / Low | ~$274 / ~$271 |
| PM Volume | Elevated |
| Catalyst | Pre-earnings positioning; broke key resistance at $265; earnings **April 30 after close** |

**Technical summary.** AAPL is the **strongest premarket performer** in today's watchlist. The stock broke above a long-term resistance zone at $265 (Triangle + 50% Fib retracement from Dec decline) on April 27, and is now gapping up further in premarket. Pre-earnings drift into Thursday's print is the primary driver. April 27 range: $265.07–$269.18 (close ~$267.40). Premarket is tagging $273.33 — a decisive breakout. VWAP on open likely near $270–271 as the stock absorbs the gap.

RSI estimated **~60–65** (not overbought, momentum building). IV elevated due to earnings proximity (estimated 35–45%).

**Support / Resistance**

| Level | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| $280–282 | Resistance | Prior swing-high zone |
| $276–278 | Resistance | Next measured target |
| $274–275 | Resistance | Near-term overhead |
| **$273.33** | Current PM price | |
| $270–271 | Support | VWAP on gap open |
| $267–268 | Support | Prev close / intraday level |
| $265 | Major Support | Breakout level — must hold |

**0DTE trade plan — see Trade Summary above.**

### MU — Micron Technology

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Intraday Range | $497.96 – **$531.33** |
| Current Price | ~$526 |
| Prev Close (est.) | ~$510 |
| Change | **+~3% intraday** |
| PM Volume | 41.3M vs 38.05M avg (above average) |
| Catalyst | NAND/AI memory momentum; SNDK sector tailwind; AI data-center demand |

**Technical summary.** MU is exhibiting strong relative strength alongside the NAND memory sector. Volume is above average. The stock is breaking to new intraday highs. Momentum-driven 0DTE CALL setup — but the wide intraday range ($33) reflects high volatility and requires careful entry management. RSI estimated ~70–74. IV estimated ~50–65%.

### PLTR — Palantir Technologies

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Open Price | **$143.09** |
| Current | ~$143.10 (+1.06%) |
| Prev Close | ~$141.59 |
| 52-Wk Range | $105.32 – $207.52 |
| Earnings | **May 4 (1 week out — no imminent risk)** |
| Analyst Target | $194.06 (+35.6%) |
| Catalyst | AI/government contracts; clean uptrend; no event risk today |

**Technical summary.** PLTR is the cleanest 0DTE setup outside of AAPL and MU. No earnings for a week, steady +1.06% gain at open, trading in a constructive uptrend from the $105 lows. RSI estimated ~57–62 (healthy momentum without overbought risk). IV estimated ~55–70%. The large gap between current price ($143) and analyst target ($194) suggests institutions are still accumulating. The 52-week high at $207 gives PLTR room to run.

### NVDA — NVIDIA Corp.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Premarket Price | $201.90 |
| Prev Close | ~$199.89 |
| Premarket Gap | **+$2.01 / +1.01%** |
| Catalyst | Record close Friday (April 25); AI infrastructure rally; RSI 72.6 |

**Technical summary.** NVDA set a record close Friday and is continuing higher in premarket. RSI is **72.6** — confirmed slightly overbought. The stock is in a clear uptrend. However, 0DTE CALL risk is higher when RSI is this elevated — reversals from overbought are swift and punishing in gamma-rich 0DTE. VWAP open likely ~$200. IV estimated 40–50%.

### SPY — S&P 500 ETF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Price | $715.17 |
| Prev Close | ~$713.94 |
| Gap % | +0.17% |
| PM Volume | High (index ETF) |
| Catalyst | Pre-earnings drift, FOMC positioning |
| VIX | 18.02 |

**Technical summary.** SPY is consolidating near the 7,150 S&P 500 equivalent. Breadth is positive. VWAP likely sits at $713–715. The trend is up from late-March lows but momentum is stalling into mega-cap earnings week. No strong directional edge intraday without a catalyst. RSI estimated ~56 (neutral).

### QQQ — Nasdaq 100 ETF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Price | $664.23 |
| Prev Close | ~$664.30 |
| Gap % | −0.01% |
| Catalyst | Mag 7 earnings tomorrow; FOMC Wednesday |
| IV est. | ~20–24% |

**Technical summary.** Essentially flat. Tech is not leading today. Pre-earnings hedging is suppressing QQQ momentum. VWAP near $664. RSI estimated ~55. No edge for 0DTE without a defined catalyst breakout.

### TSLA — Tesla Inc.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Premarket Price | **$374.12** |
| Prev Close (est.) | ~$375.62 |
| Gap | **−$1.50 / −0.40%** |
| YTD | **−16%** |
| Catalyst | Q1 revenue miss; profits beat; Cybercab/robotaxi slower than expected; capex >$25B raises cash-flow concerns |

**Technical summary.** TSLA is the weakest large-cap on the list. At -16% YTD with a revenue miss and a decelerating robotaxi roadmap, the fundamentals weigh. Technically below its 200-day MA. RSI estimated ~38–44. Potentially clean PUT setup if the broader market weakens — but with SPY +0.17%, shorting individual weak stocks against a positive tape is low conviction.

### AVOID list

#### AMD — Advanced Micro Devices
+65% April rally — now pulling back slightly with no fresh catalyst. After a sustained run, 0DTE momentum plays are risky without a clear intraday breakout. RSI estimated ~68. **Trade Grade: D+ — AVOID.**

#### AVGO — Broadcom Inc.
AI/ASIC infrastructure leader (22% YTD), but slight negative drift (-0.51%) and no immediate catalyst. **Trade Grade: D — AVOID today.**

#### GOOGL — Alphabet Inc.
Flat ahead of tomorrow's earnings. IV meaningfully elevated (~35–45%). **AVOID for 0DTE.** Earnings tomorrow = IV bloat + binary risk. Pre-earnings drift trades belong in longer-dated options, not 0DTE. **Trade Grade: D — AVOID.**

#### AMZN — Amazon
One of the strongest premarket gainers (+3.49%) but this is almost entirely pre-earnings IV expansion. 0DTE CALL options will be expensive (estimated IV 45–55%) and will expire before the earnings catalyst materializes. **Trade Grade: D — AVOID for 0DTE.**

#### META — Meta Platforms
Same earnings-tomorrow problem as GOOGL and AMZN. IV is elevated. 0DTE options will not capture the earnings move. **Trade Grade: D — AVOID.**

#### SNDK — SanDisk Corporation
Pulling back sharply in premarket (-1.99%), digesting the massive move from April 27 highs of $1,079. Earnings in 2 days means IV is extremely elevated (estimated 80–120%+). **Trade Grade: F — AVOID.** IV is potentially in triple digits. Bid-ask spreads on 0DTE options will be massive. This is a volatility trap.

| MU | 50–65% | ±$16–$22 | Strong (sector run) | Moderate | Moderate | High | **60–65%** |
| PLTR | 55–70% | ±$4.30–$5.50 | Moderate | High | Low–Mod | Moderate | **60–65%** |
| NVDA | 40–50% | ±$5.50–$7.00 | Moderate (overbought) | Very High | Low | High | **55–60%** |

| TSLA (PUT) | 55–70% | ±$11–$14 | Weak | High | Low | High | **45–52%** |
| AMZN | 45–55% | ±$8–$10 | Strong PM but IV trap | Very High | Low | Very High | **40–45%** |
| GOOGL | 35–45% | ±$8–$11 | Flat | Very High | Low | Very High | **38–42%** |
| META | 40–50% | ±$18–$22 | Flat | High | Moderate | Very High | **38–42%** |
| AMD | 40–55% | ±$9–$13 | Slightly neg | High | Low | High | **38–42%** |
| AVGO | 35–45% | ±$10–$13 | Slightly neg | High | Low | Moderate | **38–42%** |
| SNDK | 80–120%+ | ±$50–$75 | Negative PM | Low–Mod | **Very Wide** | Extreme | **30–35%** |

## Section 6 — Execution Checklist & Time Management

**Before entry (any trade):**

- SPY holding above $714? (Confirm tape is supportive)
- VIX below 19.5? (Spike above 20 = no 0DTE CALLs)
- Target ticker above VWAP?
- Premium entry within estimated zone?
- Size reduced 30–40% given pre-FOMC, pre-earnings week?

**Intraday time guidelines:**

- 09:30–09:45 → Wait, observe open, do not chase
- 09:45–11:00 → Primary entry window (best theta/gamma balance)
- 11:00–14:00 → Theta decay accelerates; only trade momentum continuation
- 14:00–14:30 → Final entry window for strong setups
- 14:30+ → EXIT OR ROLL — gamma/theta risk skyrockets
- 15:30–16:00 → 0DTE premium collapse — exit ALL positions

**Risk management:**

- Max loss per trade: 1% of account
- Max concurrent 0DTE positions: 2
- If down 20% on premium → exit immediately, no averaging

## Section 7 — Bottom Line

This is a pre-earnings, pre-FOMC Monday. The edge is narrow. Concentrate size in **AAPL** (strongest gap-and-go setup), use **MU** and **PLTR** as secondary trades, and keep powder dry for the earnings reactions Tuesday/Wednesday in longer-dated structures.
